requirements.summary: Canonical names parse ConceptPath and AuthorityPath:ConceptPath without relative or ambiguous notation.
Vocabulary versions are claims rather than semantic identity; only Shapes and Declared Fragments carry structural versions.
Actor and authority references are issuer-controlled, unambiguous, stable, and comparable within an authority domain.
An Execution explicitly presents a Capability whose holder, target, and Operation scope match the request at the target boundary.
Every effective Capability Constraint is evaluated conjunctively and unknown or failed Constraints deny before effects.
Delegation changes the holder, preserves inherited target and Operation scope, appends Constraints, and records its parent and issuer.
Primordial authority and later Genesis occurrences are bounded, enumerable, and attributable to domain policy.
The requested effect begins only after explicit target-side authority, Shape, and Constraint validation.
Rejected attempts remain observable Executions while protected command payloads are excluded from denial audit records.
Trusted target time is explicit, scoped to a time domain, monotonic where claimed, and distinct from attributable sender marks.
Events are immutable assertions that preserve emitter attribution, causation, declared Shape, and original temporal claims.
An Outcome is a terminal Event for one identifiable Execution or activity, with distinct success result and failure detail Shapes.
Shape identity is semantic and supports unit, scalar, record, sequence, choice, and opaque contracts without CLR assignability.
Same-name Shape versions are monotonic and additive; breaking structure or meaning requires a new canonical name.
Open Shapes accept valid authored Fragments through canonical projection, required Fragments fail closed, and closed Shapes reject attachment.
Cross-stack component evidence crosses an external boundary without sharing implementation-private runtime types or authority objects.
